title: 帷幕
summary:
Captain Hastings arrives at the estate via the 5:40 train and is greeted by Mrs. Lettrell and her husband, Colonel Lettrell. Mrs. Lettrell mentions that Dr. Franklin rents an underground laboratory on the property for his work with experimental animals. During their arrival, Hastings encounters Mr. Norton, a birdwatcher, and Sir William Boyd-Carrington, a decisive former provincial head in India and a skilled hunter. Sir William, who is currently angry with architects and contractors, expresses familiarity with Hastings through his connection to Poirot and makes a comment regarding Hastings' daughter, Judith.

After being shown to his room by Lestrade, Hastings finds the modern fixtures and partitioned spaces of the updated accommodations disappointing. His arrival is briefly interrupted by a sharp cry from Mrs. Lettrell calling for the Colonel.

Hastings then goes to visit Poirot and finds that the detective has been significantly changed by age and arthritis. Poirot is now thin, shriveled, and confined to a wheelchair, though he has been using hair dye to maintain a black appearance. Despite his physical decline, Poirot remains mentally sharp and maintains a warm affection for Hastings. During their meeting, Poirot complains about the poor quality of the hotel's amenities, specifically criticizing the lack of seasoning in the food, the lukewarm water, and the thin towels.
